steps.title: Recognize complementary angles
steps.content: In right triangle $RST$, angles $R$ and $S$ satisfy $R + S = 90°$, making them complementary angles. The third angle must be the $90°$ right angle.

steps.title: Apply the complementary angle identity
steps.content: For complementary angles where $R + S = 90°$, we have $\sin(R) = \cos(S)$.

steps.title: Find cos(S)
steps.content: We're given $\sin(R) = \frac{2\sqrt{10}}{7}$. Since $\sin(R) = \cos(S)$, we have $\cos(S) = \frac{2\sqrt{10}}{7}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
